data.title: Trump-Musk word wars: Billionaire Bill Ackman urges "make peace"
data.description:
The escalating war of words between U.S. President Donald Trump and Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk seems to be the end of their bromance era.  A public feud erupted between the two, attracting comments and appeals from other prominent figures in American business and politics.

The conflict intensified after Trump threatened to terminate government contracts for Musk's companies in response to his criticism of the Republican tax-cut and spending bill.

“The easiest way to save money in our Budget, Billions and Billions of Dollars, is to terminate Elon’s Governmental Subsidies and Contracts,” Trump wrote in a Truth Social post. ”I was always surprised that Biden didn’t do it!”

To this, Musk promptly responded on X, “Go ahead, make my day.” Musk also responded "Yes" to a post on X that suggested impeaching Trump. The feud got widespread attention, with many influential figures commenting, expressing hope for reconciliation, while some remain skeptical.

Billionaire hedge fund manager Bill Ackman publicly appealed both to end the bitterness. Ackman, who supported Trump in the lead-up to the 2024 election, posted on X urging the two to "make peace for the benefit of our country," emphasizing that "we are much stronger together than apart." To this, Elon Musk replied, “You’re not wrong.”

Investor Mark Cuban posted for Musk and created a poll for followers that noted, “create a new political party in America that actually represents the 80% in the middle.”

Former presidential candidate Andrew Yang reposted billionaire Mark Cuban’s comments and proposed the idea of an “Independent ’28 presidential primary.” Yang’s pitch included a list of potential participants like Mark Cuban, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon, and actor Matthew McConaughey, among others.

Steve Bannon, a former senior Trump adviser and prominent critic of Musk, took a harder stance during his “War Room Live” show on Thursday. Bannon suggested that Trump should sign an executive order to take control of SpaceX by invoking the Defense Production Act, a national security mobilization law. He argued, “The U.S. government should seize it,” and further recommended stripping Musk of his security clearance and suspending all federal contracts.

Besides, Wall Street dumped Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares, resulting in a 14% fall as investors were concerned about the Trump premium fading away. Thursday marked one of Tesla's top ten worst days ever on a percentage basis. 

ETFs to track the broader U.S. markets: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico saw 2.6% Y/Y rise in passenger traffic in May
DocuSign outlines 6% revenue growth guidance for fiscal 2026 as IAM adoption accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: DocuSign (DOCU) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Allan C. Thygesen, President and CEO, shared that "Q1 2026 was an important quarter in our long-term transformation. At our annual Momentum customer event, we announced an ambitious roadmap for Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management, the world's leading AI-driven platform. We are delivering innovation to customers at the fastest pace in our history." He emphasized strong Q1 financial performance with revenue of $764 million and 8% growth, attributing the outperformance to additional IAM customers and self-serve digital revenue. Thygesen announced an additional $1 billion in authorized share buybacks, stating that "A strong 30% free cash flow margin drove continued share repurchases and supports our conviction to authorize an additional $1 billion in buybacks."
  • Thygesen noted that foundational go-to-market changes, intended to realize IAM’s potential, resulted in lower Q1 early renewals, and that "billings growth ended slightly below our guidance range of 4% year-on-year, an outcome of timing, not demand."
  • Over 10,000 customers have purchased the IAM platform, with strong product market fit in small and mid-market segments and early promise in enterprise and self-serve organizations. International IAM deals increased by over 50% from last quarter. The launch of self-serve in April resulted in nearly 1,000 new IAM customers within three weeks.
  • Blake Jeffrey Grayson, Executive VP & CFO, stated, "Our primary goal in fiscal 2026 is to position Docusign to drive long-term growth acceleration while maintaining efficiency. In Q1, we made continued progress against this goal and delivered solid business results." Grayson highlighted that while revenue and profit margins outperformed expectations, billings came slightly below guidance due to the timing of early renewals.

Outlook

  • The company expects total revenue between $777 million and $781 million in Q2, or a 6% year-over-year increase at the midpoint, and between $3.151 billion and $3.163 billion for fiscal 2026, also a 6% year-over-year increase at the midpoint.
  • Subscription revenue is expected between $760 million and $764 million in Q2 and $3.083 billion to $3.095 billion for fiscal 2026. Billings guidance is $757 million to $767 million in Q2 and $3.285 billion to $3.339 billion for fiscal 2026. Grayson said, "For full year revenue, the annual guidance midpoint is increasing by $22 million, reflecting the combination of Q1 strength and an anticipated neutral rather than a negative year-over-year FX impact."
  • The annual billings guidance midpoint is declining by $15 million, citing additional early renewal considerations and increased bookings conservatism. Grayson explained, "While we did not see any material macro impact on our Q1 results, we are taking a cautious approach for the remainder of fiscal 2026 given the uncertain economic environment."

Financial Results

  • Q1 total revenue was $764 million and subscription revenue was $746 million, both up 8% year-over-year. Billings grew 4% year-over-year to $740 million. Non-GAAP gross margin for Q1 was 82.3%. Non-GAAP operating margin for Q1 was 29.5%.
  • Free cash flow generation was $228 million, a 30% margin. The company ended Q1 with over $1.1 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and investments, and no debt. Q1 share repurchases totaled $183 million, with up to $1.4 billion in buyback authorization now available.
  • Non-GAAP diluted EPS for Q1 was $0.90. GAAP diluted EPS for Q1 was $0.34. Large customers spending over $300,000 annually increased by 6% year-over-year to 1,123.

Q&A

  • Jacob Roberge, William Blair, asked about the go-to-market transition and lower early renewals. Thygesen responded, "We made some changes at the beginning of the first quarter to our compensation to encourage reps... We thought that that would play out during the course of the year and ended up happening predominantly here in Q1."
  • Tyler Maverick Radke, Citi, inquired about confidence in the second half billings ramp and enterprise contribution. Thygesen explained, "We were always assuming an acceleration in the second half, and that remains the case... There isn't an assumption that we're going to see some dramatic acceleration in our enterprise business in the second half."
  • Robbie David Owens, Piper Sandler, questioned if billings guidance reductions were purely timing. Thygesen confirmed, "No, I don't think we've seen anything on size of the deals... We continue to see health there."
  • Joshua Phillip Baer, Morgan Stanley, requested context on IAM’s contribution to subscription book of business. Grayson shared most IAM deals are expansions, with the biggest opportunity in the existing installed base.
  • Brent John Thill, Jefferies, asked about the magnitude and duration of the sales changes. Thygesen indicated, "I think we feel pretty good about that settle in, and we're already seeing stabilization and normalization here early in Q2."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral to slightly cautious, focusing on billings timing, the sustainability of IAM momentum, and clarity on enterprise growth contributions. Questions often sought details on the impact of go-to-market changes and early renewals, as well as confidence in guidance for the second half.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone. Thygesen consistently emphasized the strength of IAM adoption and downplayed the impact of timing-related billings effects, while acknowledging, "it's on us to have missed the forecast slightly on that." Grayson’s tone was steady, transparent about forecasting and cautious in the context of economic uncertainty.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone shifted more toward timing and sustainability of growth, while management continued to project confidence in strategic execution and IAM traction.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 revenue was $764 million compared to $776 million in Q4 2025. Billings growth slowed to 4% year-over-year from 11% in the previous quarter, primarily due to earlier-than-expected reduction in early renewals. Non-GAAP operating margin increased by 100 basis points year-over-year but was below Q4’s 29%.
  • Management’s tone remained optimistic about IAM’s growth, but acknowledged the impact of timing on billings and took a more conservative approach to forecasting early renewals. The sales force restructuring and earlier realization of renewal timing impacted short-term results.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from macroeconomic impacts and product rollout in Q4 2025 to the timing of early renewals and the sustainability of IAM-driven billings acceleration in Q1 2026.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited timing of early renewals as a primary driver for billings volatility, not underlying demand. "Billings renewal timing was impacted by the go-to-market changes discussed last quarter," Grayson said.
  • Cautious approach to guidance for the rest of fiscal 2026, factoring in uncertain macroeconomic conditions and the timing of customer renewals, though no material macro impact was seen in Q1.
  • The company acknowledged that foundational sales changes could introduce short-term disruption, but expressed confidence this had largely settled by early Q2.

Final Takeaway

DocuSign management highlighted strong Q1 performance driven by rapid IAM adoption and continued growth in digital channels. While the timing of early renewals led to billings coming in just below guidance, leadership expressed confidence in the underlying business trajectory and raised full-year revenue guidance, reflecting robust customer demand and product innovation. Strategic sales force transformations are expected to support durable long-term growth, and foundation-building in the enterprise segment remains a key focus as IAM expands its share of recurring revenue.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Broadcom in charts: FQ2 Revenue from semiconductor solutions rises by 17% Y/Y; infrastructure software jumps 25%
Rubrik raises full-year 2026 guidance with subscription ARR growth of 26%-27% as identity resilience drives platform expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Rubrik, Inc. (RBRK) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Bipul Sinha reported, "Our first quarter results were excellent. This quarter, we once again exceeded all guided metrics across top line and profitability." He highlighted subscription ARR reaching approximately $1.2 billion, a 38% year-over-year increase, and net new subscription ARR of $89 million. Subscription revenue rose to $266 million, marking 54% year-over-year growth. Sinha emphasized, "We once again made material improvements in subscription ARR contribution margin, up over 1,800 basis points year-over-year." Free cash flow surpassed $33 million for the quarter.
  • Sinha outlined Rubrik’s innovation engine, with a focus on cyber resilience and platform expansion: "Rubrik employs a unique portfolio approach to S-curves, managing innovations at different stages to maintain maximum momentum while preparing for what's next." He detailed new offerings in code-to-cloud cyber resilience, advanced protection for cloud relational databases, and expanded capabilities for Google Cloud and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. Customer wins included a major U.S. pharmacy solutions company and a large European insurer, both citing Rubrik’s rapid recovery and cloud scalability as differentiators.
  • Sinha detailed the launch and momentum of the identity recovery solution, stating, "This transforms identity recovery times from weeks to under an hour." He noted Rubrik’s approach combines data and identity context for comprehensive cyber resilience, with recent wins in healthcare and state government sectors.
  • CFO Kiran Kumar Choudary stated, "Q1 was a strong start to our fiscal year, demonstrating our continued leadership in the growing market for cyber resilience... We are pleased to have exceeded all guided metrics in Q1, and we are raising our outlook."

Outlook

  • Choudary provided updated guidance, stating, "In Q2, we expect revenue of $281 million to $283 million, up 37% to 38%. We expect non-GAAP subscription ARR contribution margins between 4.5% and 5.5%. We expect non-GAAP earnings per share of negative $0.35 to negative $0.33."
  • For the full year fiscal 2026, guidance was raised: "We expect subscription ARR in the range of $1.380 billion to $1.388 billion, reflecting a year-over-year growth rate of 26% to 27%. We expect total revenue for the full year fiscal 2026 in the range of $1.179 billion to $1.189 billion, reflecting a year-over-year growth rate of 33% to 34%." Free cash flow is expected in the range of $65 million to $75 million.
  • Management noted continued investment in R&D and go-to-market, with an emphasis on scaling new innovations and targeting high ROI regions and verticals.

Financial Results

  • Rubrik ended Q1 with subscription ARR of $1.18 billion and net new subscription ARR of $89 million. Cloud ARR was $972 million. Subscription net retention rate remained over 120%. Customers with $100,000 or more in subscription ARR reached 2,381, contributing 85% of subscription ARR.
  • Subscription revenue for the quarter was $266 million, and total revenue reached $278 million. Revenue from the Americas was $203 million, and revenue from outside the Americas was $75 million. Non-GAAP gross margin was 80.5%.
  • Subscription ARR contribution margin improved to positive 8% for the last twelve months. Free cash flow was $33 million, and Rubrik ended the quarter with $762 million in cash and $323 million in debt.

Q&A

  • Saket Kalia, Barclays: Asked about the runway for share shift from legacy to next-gen and if identity resilience expands TAM. Sinha responded that identity resilience is "expanding our TAM... we are giving our customer a peace of mind against those attacks."
  • Kasthuri Rangan, Goldman Sachs: Questioned if Rubrik is at the start of an S curve of adoption. Sinha noted, "We definitely believe that we are on the front end of a very long, large secular trend around data, security and AI."
  • Andrew Nowinski, Wells Fargo: Asked about the integration of Laminar and the potential to replace other identity security vendors. Sinha confirmed, "The first step of this strategy was to acquire DSPM Laminar product that we have fully now integrated on the Rubrik platform."
  • John DiFucci, Guggenheim: Asked about macro demand and Rubrik’s ability to execute. Sinha replied, "We are not seeing any change in our demand environment."
  • Eric Heath, KeyBanc: Sought context on the size of the identity opportunity. Sinha said, "We believe that the identity security and data security will eventually merge, and it will be a singular strategy."
  • Gregg Moskowitz, Mizuho: Asked about incremental investment in identity resilience. Sinha explained, "Identity is definitely an area of investment for us."
  • Todd Coupland, CIBC: Asked about guidance and potential conservatism. Choudary clarified, "The 7% we referred to was actually a tailwind to revenue growth in the quarter... we raised it a few points in terms of total growth."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on runway for next-gen displacement, the ramp-up of identity resilience, and adoption cycles for Rubrik’s platform, displaying a slightly positive tone but probing for clarity on long-term opportunity and guidance rationale.
  • Management maintained a confident, forward-looking tone during prepared remarks, using terms such as "we remain confident" and "the best is yet to come." In Q&A, Sinha and Choudary were constructive and assertive, frequently detailing competitive advantages and market expansion, though Sinha occasionally used clarifying language such as "as I said before" when emphasizing differentiation.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management demonstrated increased focus and optimism around identity resilience as a growth driver, while analysts’ tone shifted from cautious optimism to more direct interest in new product contributions and TAM expansion.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for full-year subscription ARR increased to a range of $1.380 billion to $1.388 billion, up from $1.350 billion to $1.360 billion previously. Full-year revenue guidance increased from a prior range of $1.145 billion to $1.161 billion to a new range of $1.179 billion to $1.189 billion.
  • Net new subscription ARR for Q1 was $89 million, compared to over $90 million in Q4. The number of customers with $100,000 or more in subscription ARR rose from 2,246 to 2,381. Non-GAAP gross margin increased from 80% in Q4 to 80.5% in Q1.
  • Management’s tone in Q1 was more assertively focused on identity resilience as a new platform pillar, while analysts increasingly centered their questions on the integration, adoption, and impact of identity-related products relative to previous quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management referenced seasonality in subscription ARR contribution margins and the timing of net new subscription ARR and operating expenses. Choudary noted, "We continue to anticipate that subscription contribution margins will be the seasonally lowest in Q3 before moving higher in Q4."
  • Choudary also cited shorter contract terms and an increasing mix of annual and monthly payment terms as factors impacting free cash flow, though higher sales and operating leverage offset these trends.
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of elevated net retention rates and the pace of adoption for new identity solutions, with management acknowledging early-stage momentum and the need for ongoing investment in innovation and go-to-market.

Final Takeaway

Rubrik’s Q1 2026 performance highlighted strong momentum in subscription ARR and profitability, driven by expanding platform capabilities in cyber resilience and the rapid adoption of identity recovery solutions. Management raised full-year guidance for both ARR and revenue, signaling continued confidence in market demand and product differentiation. The company’s innovation in combining data and identity security was repeatedly underscored as a unique market advantage and key growth lever for the year ahead.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund considers involvement in $7 billion Grab-GoTo deal - report
data.description:
Newly launched sovereign wealth fund Danantara Indonesia is in early talks with GoTo (OTCPK:GTOFF) to get a piece of U.S.-listed rival Grab's (NASDAQ:GRAB) potential buyout of the ride-hailing and food delivery firm, Bloomberg reported on Friday.

The fund is seeking a minority stake in the combined entity, which could help alleviate the Indonesian government's concerns about Singapore-headquartered Grab (NASDAQ:GRAB) owning the country's largest tech firm, the report said.

Last month, Indonesia's antitrust regulator launched a study to assess potential risks arising from a possible merger between the tech giants, although the companies have not yet confirmed any merger discussions.

The companies have made progress on the deal's structure, but talks have slowed down recently due to potential regulatory demands, Bloomberg News reported.

Grab, backed by Uber Technologies (UBER), is looking to strike a deal in the second quarter and could value GoTo at around $7 billion, sources with knowledge of the matter told Reuters last month.

In February, Indonesia launched Danantara, an investment entity designed to fund a broad spectrum of projects, ranging from metal processing to artificial intelligence. It will manage government stakes in state-owned companies and is modeled after Singapore’s investment arm, Temasek.

data.title: Asia markets mixed post-Trump-Xi call; U.S. futures steady ahead of May jobs report
data.description:
Asia stock markets trade mixed on Friday, tracking a downbeat session on Wall Street overnight, weighed down by a dispute between Trump and Elon Musk, and renewed US-China trade uncertainty.

Gold rose to around $3,370 per ounce on Friday, climbing back to a four-week-high.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 0.37% rose 0.4% to above 37,600 while the broader Topix Index gained 0.4% to 2,767 on Friday, reversing losses from the previous session. The Japanese yen slipped toward 144 per dollar on Friday, extending losses from the previous session.

On the domestic front, data showed that Japan’s household spending unexpectedly declined in April, as rising prices continued to weigh on consumer demand. 

Japan’s foreign reserves fell slightly by $110 million to $1.298 trillion in May 2025, remaining close to the highest level since mid-2022.

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.06% traded around the flat line on Friday, struggling for clear direction as investors assessed the phone call between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, and the offshore yuan weakened toward 7.18 per dollar on Friday, extending losses from the previous session.

President Trump and China’s Xi Jinping spoke on Thursday and agreed that officials from both sides would soon resume negotiations aimed at ending the trade war, though no concrete details were provided. 

Investors now turn their attention to upcoming Chinese inflation and trade data, due Monday, for fresh signals on the health of the world’s second-largest economy. 

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 0.09% to 23,834 in early trade on Friday, halting gains from the previous session.

India (SENSEX) rose 0.18% The Reserve Bank of India lowered its key repo rate by 50 basis points to 5.50% at its May meeting, marking the third consecutive monthly rate cut.

Australia (AS51) fell 0.14% nearly flat to around 8,540 in early trading Friday, but on track for a fourth weekly gain. The Australian dollar slipped toward $0.650 on Friday, ending a two-day winning streak, amid cautious market sentiment.

Private house approvals in Australia increased by 3.1% month-over-month to a seven-month high of 9,349 units in April 2025.

The seasonally adjusted number of total dwellings approved in Australia fell 5.7% month-over-month to an eight-month low of 14,633 units in April 2025.

In the U.S., on Thursday, all three major indexes ended lower as investors reacted to a public disagreement between President Trump and Elon Musk, renewed U.S.-China trade uncertainty, and increasing signs of a weakening labor market.

U.S. stock futures stabilized on Friday as investors awaited the closely watched May jobs report, expected to shed light on the state of the labor market: Dow +0.32%; S&P 500 +0.29%; Nasdaq +0.23%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

Australia's trade surplus narrows in April to AUD 5.41B on weaker exports, higher imports

China's services sector growth accelerates in May amid stronger business & activity

Australia's Q1 GDP misses estimates amid weak public spending, consumer demand, and exports

Australia's manufacturing stalls at -23.5 in May, services sector continues 16-month growth

Japan services PMI revised upward to 51.0 in May 2025

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Employment Situation

Nonfarm payrolls track the number of part-time and full-time employees in both business and government. Economists forecast 129K jobs to added during the May month, and for the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.2%.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs. The active rig count acts as a leading indicator of demand for oil products. The active rig count acts as a leading indicator of demand for oil products. Crude Oil Rigs in the United States fell to 461 in May 30 week from 465 in the previous week.

3:00 PM Consumer Credit  Changes in consumer credit indicate the state of consumer finances and portend future spending patterns. The report includes credit cards, vehicle loans, and student loans; mortgages are not included. 

An identical, trend-like increase of $10.2 billion expected in April after a gain of $10.2 billion in March.
